CAVEMAN MODE ACTIVE — level: full

Respond terse like smart caveman. All technical substance stay. Only fluff die.

## Persistence

ACTIVE EVERY RESPONSE. No revert after many turns. No filler drift. Still active if unsure. Off only: "stop caveman" / "normal mode".

Default: **full**. Switch: `/caveman lite|full|ultra`.

## Rules

Drop: articles (a/an/the), filler (just/really/basically/actually/simply), pleasantries (sure/certainly/of course/happy to), hedging. Fragments OK. Short synonyms (big not extensive, fix not "implement a solution for"). Technical terms exact. Code blocks unchanged. Errors quoted exact.

Pattern: `[thing] [action] [reason]. [next step].`

Not: "Sure! I'd be happy to help you with that. The issue you're experiencing is likely caused by..."
Yes: "Bug in auth middleware. Token expiry check use `<` not `<=`. Fix:"

## Intensity

| Level | What change |
|-------|------------|
| **full** | Drop articles, fragments OK, short synonyms. Classic caveman |

Example — "Why React component re-render?"
- full: "New object ref each render. Inline object prop = new ref = re-render. Wrap in `useMemo`."

Example — "Explain database connection pooling."
- full: "Pool reuse open DB connections. No new connection per request. Skip handshake overhead."

## Auto-Clarity

Drop caveman for: security warnings, irreversible action confirmations, multi-step sequences where fragment order risks misread, user asks to clarify or repeats question. Resume caveman after clear part done.

Example — destructive op:
> **Warning:** This will permanently delete all rows in the `users` table and cannot be undone.
> ```sql
> DROP TABLE users;
> ```
> Caveman resume. Verify backup exist first.

## Boundaries

Code/commits/PRs: write normal. "stop caveman" or "normal mode": revert. Level persist until changed or session end.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- ship: Review all uncommitted changes, write a commit message, commit, and push — no co-author line.
- caveman:caveman-commit: Ultra-compressed commit message generator. Cuts noise from commit messages while preserving intent and reasoning. Conventional Commits format. Subject ≤50 chars, body only when "why" isn't obvious. Use when user says "write a commit", "commit message", "generate commit", "/commit", or invokes /caveman-commit. Auto-triggers when staging changes.
- caveman:caveman-help: Quick-reference card for all caveman modes, skills, and commands. One-shot display, not a persistent mode. Trigger: /caveman-help, "caveman help", "what caveman commands", "how do I use caveman".
- caveman:caveman-review: Ultra-compressed code review comments. Cuts noise from PR feedback while preserving the actionable signal. Each comment is one line: location, problem, fix. Use when user says "review this PR", "code review", "review the diff", "/review", or invokes /caveman-review. Auto-triggers when reviewing pull requests.
- caveman:caveman: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by speaking like caveman while keeping full technical accuracy. Supports intensity levels: lite, full (default), ultra, wenyan-lite, wenyan-full, wenyan-ultra. Use when user says "caveman mode", "talk like caveman", "use caveman", "less tokens", "be brief", or invokes /caveman. Also auto-triggers when token efficiency is requested.
- caveman:compress: Compress natural language memory files (CLAUDE.md, todos, preferences) into caveman format to save input tokens. Preserves all technical substance, code, URLs, and structure. Compressed version overwrites the original file. Human-readable backup saved as FILE.original.md. Trigger: /caveman:compress <filepath> or "compress memory file"
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
# frozen_string_literal: true

# =============================================================================
# INFLECTIONS - Pluralization and Acronym Rules
# =============================================================================
# Rails automatically converts between singular/plural and different cases:
#   "user" -> "users" (pluralization)
#   "user_name" -> "UserName" (camelization)
#
# Problem: Rails doesn't know about acronyms like API, JWT, HTML, etc.
# Without this config:
#   "api" -> "Api" (wrong!)
#   "jwt_service" -> "JwtService" (wrong!)
#
# With this config:
#   "api" -> "API" (correct!)
#   "jwt_service" -> "JWTService" (correct!)

ActiveSupport::Inflector.inflections(:en) do |inflect|
  # Define acronyms that should stay uppercase
  inflect.acronym 'API'  # For: Api::V1 -> API::V1
  inflect.acronym 'JWT'  # For: JwtService -> JWTService
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

# =============================================================================
# AUTHENTICATION CONTROLLER
# =============================================================================
# Handles all authentication operations:
# - register: Create new user account
# - login: Authenticate and issue tokens
# - logout: Revoke refresh token and current access token (JTI)
# - refresh: Exchange refresh token for new access token
# - validate: Verify access token (for other microservices)
# - me: Get current user info
# - change_password: Change authenticated user's password
# - forgot_password: Request password reset email
# - reset_password: Reset password via token
# - verify_email: Confirm email address via token
#
# SECURITY PRINCIPLES:
# 1. Generic error messages (don't reveal if email exists)
# 2. Timing-safe comparisons (bcrypt handles this)
# 3. Rate limiting (handled by Rack::Attack middleware)
# 4. Token validation on protected endpoints
# 5. JTI-based access token revocation on logout and password changes

module Api
  module V1
    class AuthenticationController < ApplicationController
      # ========================================================================
      # BEFORE ACTIONS
      # ========================================================================

# frozen_string_literal: true

# Publishes events to RabbitMQ for async processing by notification service.
#
# Usage:
#   EventPublisher.publish('email.password_reset', {
#     event_type: 'email.password_reset',
#     payload: { to: 'user@example.com', data: { ... } }
#   })
#
# Events are published to a topic exchange named 'notifications'.
# Routing keys follow the pattern: email.<type> (e.g., email.password_reset)
#
# Connection strategy: one persistent connection per process, shared across
# requests. Channels are created per publish call (cheap; no TCP handshake).
# Bunny auto-recovery handles transient RabbitMQ blips transparently.
class EventPublisher
  EXCHANGE_NAME = 'notifications'
  CONNECTION_MUTEX = Mutex.new
  private_constant :CONNECTION_MUTEX

  class << self
    def publish(routing_key, payload)
      unless enabled?
        log_disabled_warning(routing_key)
        return false
      end

      publish_to_rabbitmq(routing_key, payload)
      log_success(routing_key)
      true
    rescue StandardError => e
      log_failure(routing_key, e)
      false
    end

    def enabled?
      ENV.fetch('RABBITMQ_ENABLED', 'true') == 'true'
    end

    # Resets the persistent connection (used in tests and after fork).
    def reset_connection!
      CONNECTION_MUTEX.synchronize do
        begin
          @connection&.close
        rescue StandardError
          nil
        end
        @connection = nil
      end
    end

    private

    def publish_to_rabbitmq(routing_key, payload)
      with_channel do |channel|
        exchange = channel.topic(EXCHANGE_NAME, durable: true)
        exchange.publish(payload.to_json, message_options(routing_key))
      end
    end

    def message_options(routing_key)
      {
        routing_key: routing_key,
        persistent: true,
        content_type: 'application/json',
        timestamp: Time.current.to_i
      }
    end

    # Reuses the persistent connection; creates it on first call or after recovery.
    # Thread-safe: double-checked locking with CONNECTION_MUTEX.
    def connection
      return @connection if @connection&.open?

      CONNECTION_MUTEX.synchronize do
        return @connection if @connection&.open?

        conn = Bunny.new(connection_options)
        conn.start
        @connection = conn
      end
    end

    def with_channel
      channel = connection.create_channel
      yield channel
    ensure
      channel&.close
    end

    def connection_options
      {
        host: ENV.fetch('RABBITMQ_HOST', 'localhost'),
        port: ENV.fetch('RABBITMQ_PORT', 5672).to_i,
        user: ENV.fetch('RABBITMQ_USER', 'guest'),
        password: ENV.fetch('RABBITMQ_PASSWORD', 'guest'),
        vhost: ENV.fetch('RABBITMQ_VHOST', '/'),
        automatically_recover: true
      }
    end

    def log_disabled_warning(routing_key)
      Rails.logger.warn "[EventPublisher] RabbitMQ disabled, skipping: #{routing_key}"
    end

    def log_success(routing_key)
      Rails.logger.info "[EventPublisher] Published #{routing_key}"
    end

    def log_failure(routing_key, error)
      Rails.logger.error "[EventPublisher] Failed to publish #{routing_key}: #{error.message}"
    end
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

require 'rails_helper'

RSpec.describe EventPublisher do
  describe '.publish' do
    let(:routing_key) { 'email.password_reset' }
    let(:payload) do
      {
        event_type: 'email.password_reset',
        timestamp: Time.current.iso8601,
        payload: {
          to: 'user@example.com',
          data: { reset_url: 'https://example.com/reset?token=abc' }
        }
      }
    end

    context 'when RabbitMQ is disabled' do
      before do
        allow(ENV).to receive(:fetch).and_call_original
        allow(ENV).to receive(:fetch).with('RABBITMQ_ENABLED', 'true').and_return('false')
        allow(Rails.logger).to receive(:warn)
      end

      it 'returns false' do
        expect(described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)).to be false
      end

      it 'logs a warning' do
        described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)
        expect(Rails.logger).to have_received(:warn).with(/RabbitMQ disabled/)
      end

      it 'does not attempt to connect' do
        allow(Bunny).to receive(:new)
        described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)
        expect(Bunny).not_to have_received(:new)
      end
    end

    context 'when RabbitMQ is enabled' do
      let(:mock_connection) { instance_double(Bunny::Session) }
      let(:mock_channel) { instance_double(Bunny::Channel) }
      let(:mock_exchange) { instance_double(Bunny::Exchange) }

      before do
        allow(ENV).to receive(:fetch).and_call_original
        allow(ENV).to receive(:fetch).with('RABBITMQ_ENABLED', 'true').and_return('true')
        allow(Bunny).to receive(:new).and_return(mock_connection)
        allow(mock_connection).to receive(:start)
        allow(mock_connection).to receive(:create_channel).and_return(mock_channel)
        allow(mock_connection).to receive(:close)
        allow(mock_channel).to receive(:topic).and_return(mock_exchange)
        allow(mock_exchange).to receive(:publish)
        allow(Rails.logger).to receive(:info)
      end

      it 'returns true on success' do
        expect(described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)).to be true
      end

      it 'publishes to the notifications exchange' do
        described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)
        expect(mock_channel).to have_received(:topic).with('notifications', durable: true)
      end

      it 'publishes with correct routing key' do
        described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)
        expect(mock_exchange).to have_received(:publish).with(
          anything,
          hash_including(routing_key: 'email.password_reset')
        )
      end

      it 'publishes persistent messages' do
        described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)
        expect(mock_exchange).to have_received(:publish).with(
          anything,
          hash_including(persistent: true)
        )
      end

      it 'publishes JSON content' do
        described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)
        expect(mock_exchange).to have_received(:publish).with(
          payload.to_json,
          hash_including(content_type: 'application/json')
        )
      end

      it 'closes the connection after publishing' do
        described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)
        expect(mock_connection).to have_received(:close)
      end
    end

    context 'when connection fails' do
      before do
        allow(ENV).to receive(:fetch).and_call_original
        allow(ENV).to receive(:fetch).with('RABBITMQ_ENABLED', 'true').and_return('true')
        allow(Bunny).to receive(:new).and_raise(Bunny::TCPConnectionFailed.new('Connection refused'))
        allow(Rails.logger).to receive(:error)
      end

      it 'returns false' do
        expect(described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)).to be false
      end

      it 'logs the error' do
        described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)
        expect(Rails.logger).to have_received(:error).with(/Failed to publish/)
      end
    end
  end

  describe '.enabled?' do
    context 'when RABBITMQ_ENABLED is true' do
      before do
        allow(ENV).to receive(:fetch).with('RABBITMQ_ENABLED', 'true').and_return('true')
      end

      it 'returns true' do
        expect(described_class.enabled?).to be true
      end
    end

    context 'when RABBITMQ_ENABLED is false' do
      before do
        allow(ENV).to receive(:fetch).with('RABBITMQ_ENABLED', 'true').and_return('false')
      end

      it 'returns false' do
        expect(described_class.enabled?).to be false
      end
    end

    context 'when RABBITMQ_ENABLED is not set' do
      before do
        allow(ENV).to receive(:fetch).with('RABBITMQ_ENABLED', 'true').and_return('true')
      end

      it 'defaults to true' do
        expect(described_class.enabled?).to be true
      end
    end
  end
end

        payload: {
          to: 'user@example.com',
          data: { reset_url: 'https://example.com/reset?token=abc' }

      }

        allow(ENV).to receive(:fetch).and_call_original

      end

require 'rails_helper'

RSpec.describe EventPublisher do
  after { described_class.reset_connection! }

  describe '.publish' do
    let(:routing_key) { 'email.password_reset' }
    let(:payload) do
      {
        event_type: 'email.password_reset',
        timestamp: Time.current.iso8601,
        payload: {
          to: 'user@example.com',
          data: { reset_url: 'https://example.com/reset?token=abc' }
        }
      }
    end

    context 'when RabbitMQ is disabled' do
      before do
        allow(ENV).to receive(:fetch).and_call_original
        allow(ENV).to receive(:fetch).with('RABBITMQ_ENABLED', 'true').and_return('false')
        allow(Rails.logger).to receive(:warn)
      end

      it 'returns false' do
        expect(described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)).to be false
      end

      it 'logs a warning' do
        described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)
        expect(Rails.logger).to have_received(:warn).with(/RabbitMQ disabled/)
      end

      it 'does not attempt to connect' do
        allow(Bunny).to receive(:new)
        described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)
        expect(Bunny).not_to have_received(:new)
      end
    end

    context 'when RabbitMQ is enabled' do
      let(:mock_connection) { instance_double(Bunny::Session) }
      let(:mock_channel) { instance_double(Bunny::Channel) }
      let(:mock_exchange) { instance_double(Bunny::Exchange) }

      before do
        allow(ENV).to receive(:fetch).and_call_original
        allow(ENV).to receive(:fetch).with('RABBITMQ_ENABLED', 'true').and_return('true')
        allow(Bunny).to receive(:new).and_return(mock_connection)
        allow(mock_connection).to receive(:start)
        allow(mock_connection).to receive(:create_channel).and_return(mock_channel)
        allow(mock_connection).to receive(:close)
        allow(mock_channel).to receive(:topic).and_return(mock_exchange)
        allow(mock_exchange).to receive(:publish)
        allow(Rails.logger).to receive(:info)
      end

      it 'returns true on success' do
        expect(described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)).to be true
      end

      it 'publishes to the notifications exchange' do
        described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)
        expect(mock_channel).to have_received(:topic).with('notifications', durable: true)
      end

      it 'publishes with correct routing key' do
        described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)
        expect(mock_exchange).to have_received(:publish).with(
          anything,
          hash_including(routing_key: 'email.password_reset')
        )
      end

      it 'publishes persistent messages' do
        described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)
        expect(mock_exchange).to have_received(:publish).with(
          anything,
          hash_including(persistent: true)
        )
      end

      it 'publishes JSON content' do
        described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)
        expect(mock_exchange).to have_received(:publish).with(
          payload.to_json,
          hash_including(content_type: 'application/json')
        )
      end

      it 'closes the connection after publishing' do
        described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)
        expect(mock_connection).to have_received(:close)
      end
    end

    context 'when connection fails' do
      before do
        allow(ENV).to receive(:fetch).and_call_original
        allow(ENV).to receive(:fetch).with('RABBITMQ_ENABLED', 'true').and_return('true')
        allow(Bunny).to receive(:new).and_raise(Bunny::TCPConnectionFailed.new('Connection refused'))
        allow(Rails.logger).to receive(:error)
      end

      it 'returns false' do
        expect(described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)).to be false
      end

      it 'logs the error' do
        described_class.publish(routing_key, payload)
        expect(Rails.logger).to have_received(:error).with(/Failed to publish/)
      end
    end
  end

  describe '.enabled?' do
    context 'when RABBITMQ_ENABLED is true' do
      before do
        allow(ENV).to receive(:fetch).with('RABBITMQ_ENABLED', 'true').and_return('true')
      end

      it 'returns true' do
        expect(described_class.enabled?).to be true
      end
    end

    context 'when RABBITMQ_ENABLED is false' do
      before do
        allow(ENV).to receive(:fetch).with('RABBITMQ_ENABLED', 'true').and_return('false')
      end

      it 'returns false' do
        expect(described_class.enabled?).to be false
      end
    end

    context 'when RABBITMQ_ENABLED is not set' do
      before do
        allow(ENV).to receive(:fetch).with('RABBITMQ_ENABLED', 'true').and_return('true')
      end

      it 'defaults to true' do
        expect(described_class.enabled?).to be true
      end
    end
  end
end

**Fixes applied:**

2. `spec/services/event_publisher_spec.rb` — three fixes:
   - Added `after { described_class.reset_connection! }` — clears cached `@connection` between examples (was leaking stale mock doubles)
   - Added `allow(mock_channel).to receive(:close)` — service's `with_channel` ensure block calls `channel&.close`, was unstubbed

Review all uncommitted changes, write a commit message, commit, and push — no co-author line.

## Steps

1. Run these in parallel:
   - `git status` — see untracked and modified files
   - `git diff` — see unstaged changes
   - `git diff --cached` — see staged changes
   - `git log -5 --oneline` — learn commit message style of this repo

2. Analyze all changes. Understand what changed and why (infer from context).

3. Stage changed files by name (not `git add -A` or `git add .` — be precise, skip anything that looks like secrets or large binaries).

4. Write a commit message:
   - First line: imperative mood, under 72 chars, lowercase verb (e.g. "add", "fix", "refactor")
   - Body if needed: explain the why, not the what
   - **No `Co-Authored-By` line. No Claude attribution. Nothing about AI.**

5. Commit using a HEREDOC so formatting is preserved:
   ```
   git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
   <message here>
   EOF
   )"
   ```

6. Push to the current branch's remote tracking branch:
   ```
   git push
   ```
   If no upstream set: `git push -u origin <branch>`.

7. Report: what was committed, the commit hash, push result. One short paragraph, no fluff.

## Constraints

- Never use `--no-verify`
- Never add `Co-Authored-By: Claude` or any AI attribution
- Never commit `.env`, credential files, or secrets
- If nothing to commit, say so and stop

All commits must use **Conventional Commits** format:

```
<type>(<scope>): <subject>

```

**Types:** `feat`, `fix`, `chore`, `refactor`, `test`, `docs`, `style`, `perf`, `ci`, `build`

- Subject: imperative, lowercase, no period, ≤72 chars
- Scope: optional, use service/area name (e.g. `auth`, `jwt`, `rack-attack`)
- Body: explain *why*, not *what* — omit if subject is self-explanatory

fix(controller): correct module namespace from Api to API
test(event-publisher): reset class-level connection between examples

Infrastructure: PostgreSQL 17 (two instances: auth on 5432, core on 5433), Redis 7.4, RabbitMQ 4.0, MailHog, Nginx reverse proxy on ports 80/443.

## Infrastructure / Docker

```bash
cd infrastructure
./start-all.sh              # Start all services (recommended)
./start-infrastructure.sh   # Start only infra (DB, cache, broker)
./stop-all.sh               # Stop everything
docker-compose logs -f [service]
```

Dev access points when running:
- Auth API: http://localhost:3000 — Swagger docs at http://localhost:3000/api-docs
- Admin: http://localhost:3001
- RabbitMQ UI: http://localhost:15672
- MailHog: http://localhost:8025

## Auth API (Ruby/Rails)

```bash
cd auth-api
bundle install
cd ../infrastructure && docker-compose up auth-api   # Start auth-api in Docker
bundle exec rspec                                          # All tests
bundle exec rspec spec/models/auth_credential_spec.rb     # Single file
COVERAGE=true bundle exec rspec                           # With coverage
bundle exec rubocop                                        # Lint
bundle exec rubocop -A                                     # Auto-fix
bundle exec rake rswag:specs:swaggerize                   # Regenerate OpenAPI docs
bundle exec rails db:create db:migrate
bundle exec rake tokens:cleanup                            # Maintenance task
```

### Auth API Architecture

- **Routes**: `config/routes.rb` → all endpoints under `/api/v1/auth/`
- **Controllers**: `app/controllers/api/v1/` — thin, delegate to services
- **Services**: `app/services/` — business logic layer (password policy, HIBP check, token management)
- **Models**: `AuthCredential` (users), `RefreshToken`, `PasswordHistory`, `PasswordResetToken`
- **JWT**: 15-min access tokens, 8-hour refresh tokens (30-min idle timeout)
- **Security**: NIST SP 800-63B password compliance, HIBP breach detection, rack-attack rate limiting, password history enforcement
- **Async**: RabbitMQ publishes to `notifications` topic exchange (routing key `email.password_reset`) — consumed by `notification-service`
- **Linting config**: `.rubocop.yml`

## Admin Dashboard (React/TypeScript)

```bash
cd admin
npm install
npm run dev       # Vite dev server on port 3001
npm run build     # TypeScript check + Vite production build
npm run lint      # ESLint
npm run preview   # Preview production build
```

### Admin Architecture

- **Entry**: `src/main.tsx` → `src/App.tsx` (React Router v7 routes)
- **Pages**: `src/pages/` — `login.tsx`, `dashboard.tsx`
- **API clients**: `src/api/client.ts` (Axios), `src/api/auth.ts`
- **Auth state**: `src/contexts/auth-context.tsx`
- **Guards**: `src/components/protected-route.tsx`
- **UI**: shadcn/ui components; forms use react-hook-form + Zod

## Notification Service (Go)

Background worker that consumes email events from RabbitMQ and sends emails via MailHog (dev) / SMTP (prod).

```bash
cd notification-service
go build ./...
go vet ./...
# Run via Docker (no standalone dev server):
cd ../infrastructure && docker-compose up notification-service
```

### Notification Service Architecture

- **Entry**: `cmd/main.go` — starts consumer with retry loop (up to 10 attempts, 5s delay)
- **Consumer**: `internal/consumer/consumer.go` — connects to RabbitMQ, binds queue `notification-service.email` to `notifications` exchange with routing key `email.*`
- **Handlers**: `internal/handlers/password_reset.go` — handles `email.password_reset` event type
- **Mailer**: `internal/mailer/mailer.go` — sends email via SMTP

## Dev Setup

Scripts in `dev-setup/` for bootstrapping a local multi-repo environment:

```bash
cd dev-setup
./repos-setup.sh    # Clone all service repos
./repos-update.sh   # Pull latest changes across all repos
./tmux-repos.sh     # Launch tmux sessions per repo with dev windows
```

## Container-First Rule

**Never install dependencies on the local machine.** All runtimes, gems, packages, and tools run inside Docker containers.

- No `bundle install`, `npm install`, `go get` locally
- No local `ruby`, `node`, `go` version management (rbenv, nvm, etc.) for running code
- Run commands via `docker-compose exec <service> <cmd>` or inside the container
- Tests, linters, migrations — all through Docker

```bash
# Run rspec inside container
docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rspec

# Run rubocop inside container
docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rubocop

# Rails tasks inside container
docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rails db:migrate
```

### Updating Gemfile.lock (Ruby)

After editing `Gemfile`, regenerate `Gemfile.lock` on disk using the image before rebuilding — never skip this step or bundler will error on mismatch.

```bash
# Run BEFORE docker compose build whenever Gemfile changes
docker run --rm -v /path/to/auth-api:/app infrastructure-auth-api bundle lock

# Then rebuild
docker compose build auth-api
```

## API Testing

Bruno collections in `bruno/auth-api/` — use Bruno (not Postman) for manual API testing. Environments defined in `bruno/auth-api/environments/`.

## New Endpoint Rule

After adding any new endpoint to auth-api, always:

1. Write a rswag integration spec in `spec/integration/authentication_spec.rb` documenting the endpoint
2. Regenerate OpenAPI docs:
```bash
docker-compose exec auth-api bundle exec rake rswag:specs:swaggerize
```

Never ship a new endpoint without updating the OpenAPI spec.
